body:
### Overview

This control identifies cases where an object container or bucket can be accessed through a serverless function that has an unauthenticated HTTP trigger. The check follows relationships from unauthenticated functions to AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage, Azure Blob containers, or Alibaba OSS buckets.

Fix the unauthenticated function trigger, the function's storage permissions, or the bucket policy to break the path.
name: Ensure buckets are not accessible from functions with http trigger unauthenticated
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Break the unauthenticated path from a public HTTP-triggered function to object storage. The check verifies for buckets or blob containers reachable through a function that accepts unauthenticated HTTP requests.

### AWS

1. Identify the Lambda function and S3 bucket shown in the finding.
2. If the function uses a Lambda Function URL, set AuthType to AWS_IAM.
3. If the function is behind API Gateway, require an authorizer, IAM authorization, or another approved authentication mechanism.
4. Remove broad S3 permissions from the Lambda execution role; grant only required bucket actions and object prefixes.
5. If the function must stay public, move object access behind application authorization and do not let anonymous requests directly trigger storage access.

Set Function URL auth to IAM:

~~~bash
aws lambda update-function-url-config \
  --function-name <function-name> \
  --auth-type AWS_IAM \
  --region {{asset.region}}
~~~

Review role policies:

~~~bash
aws lambda get-function-configuration --function-name <function-name> --region {{asset.region}}
aws iam list-attached-role-policies --role-name <lambda-execution-role>
~~~

References:
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/urls-auth.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/lambda-intro-execution-role.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/security-iam.html

The control is satisfied when the S3 bucket is no longer reachable through an unauthenticated HTTP-triggered function.

### GCP

1. Identify the Cloud Function or Cloud Run service and the Cloud Storage bucket in the finding.
2. Remove allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers from the function or Cloud Run invoker policy.
3. Require IAM authentication, Identity-Aware Proxy, API Gateway auth, or application-level authorization before storage access.
4. Remove broad Storage roles from the function service account and grant only the required bucket/object permissions.
5. Validate invoker and bucket IAM policies.

Remove public invoker access for Cloud Run-backed functions/services:

~~~bash
gcloud run services remove-iam-policy-binding <service-name> \
  --member="allUsers" \
  --role="roles/run.invoker" \
  --region <region>
~~~

Review bucket IAM:

~~~bash
gcloud storage buckets get-iam-policy gs://<bucket-name>
~~~

References:
- https://cloud.google.com/functions/docs/securing/authenticating
- https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/authenticating/public
- https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-control/iam

The control is satisfied when the bucket is not accessible through an unauthenticated function path.

### Azure

1. Identify the Azure Function and Storage container in the finding.
2. Change HTTP trigger authorization from anonymous to function, admin, or an authenticated App Service Authentication flow.
3. Enable App Service Authentication when user or workload identity should be enforced.
4. Remove broad Storage Blob Data roles from the function identity; grant only required container-level permissions.
5. If the function exposes a public API, validate authorization before reading from or writing to Blob Storage.

Check function auth settings:

~~~bash
az webapp auth show \
  --resource-group <resource-group> \
  --name <function-app-name>
~~~

Enable authentication and reject unauthenticated requests:

~~~bash
az webapp auth update \
  --resource-group <resource-group> \
  --name <function-app-name> \
  --enabled true \
  --unauthenticated-client-action Return401
~~~

References:
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/functions-bindings-http-webhook-trigger
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/overview-authentication-authorization
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/assign-azure-role-data-access

The control is satisfied when Blob Storage is not reachable through an unauthenticated HTTP-triggered function.

### Alibaba

1. Identify the Function Compute function, HTTP trigger, and OSS bucket in the finding.
2. Require authentication for the HTTP trigger or place the function behind an authenticated API Gateway route.
3. Remove broad OSS permissions from the function service role.
4. Grant only the required OSS actions for the specific bucket and prefix.
5. Validate the trigger authentication type and RAM policies before re-running the assessment.

Review function triggers and role policies:

~~~bash
aliyun fc-open GET /services/<service-name>/functions/<function-name>/triggers
aliyun ram ListPoliciesForRole --RoleName <function-role-name>
~~~

References:
- https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/en/functioncompute/fc-3-0/user-guide/configure-an-http-trigger
- https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/en/oss/user-guide/use-ram-policies-to-control-access-to-oss

The control is satisfied when OSS buckets are no longer reachable through unauthenticated HTTP-triggered functions.
description: Object storage buckets reachable from unauthenticated HTTP-triggered functions.
